question.text: What is the main difference between the Bacteria and Archaea domains?
question.option: Bacteria are multicellular while Archaea are unicellular.
Bacteria have cell walls with peptidoglycan, whereas Archaea have cell walls without peptidoglycan.
Bacteria are eukaryotic while Archaea are prokaryotic.
Bacteria can photosynthesize while Archaea cannot.

question.text: If an organism is a multicellular autotroph and has a cell wall, to which kingdom does it belong?
You learned that a unicellular organism has chloroplasts and a nucleus. To which kingdom does it belong?
Which of the following kingdoms consists primarily of multicellular heterotrophs with no cell walls or chloroplasts?
Which of the following is an example of an organism from the Eubacteria domain?
